layman_headline: Extreme fear grips crypto as geopolitical tensions and inflation fears keep traders on the sidelines.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in 'Extreme Fear' mode with a score of just 22 out of 100. Bitcoin is wobbling around $62,500 while Iran conflict headlines and an upcoming inflation report are making everyone nervous. The U.S. government just moved nearly $300 million in seized crypto to Coinbase, which often spooks markets. None of the altcoin candidates show a clean enough setup to justify risking money right now — most are in downtrends with bearish chart structures and high BTC dominance is crushing alt performance.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market is fearful for good reasons — geopolitical risk and inflation uncertainty. A better entry will come once we see fear stabilize or a clear reversal pattern form. Patience here protects your capital.
expert_reasoning: Every candidate has significant flaws: GRASS is down 32% in 7 days but RSI is only 47 (not oversold enough for a bounce trade) with a -0.45 chart bias and poor 0.69x R/R. BONK has RSI 33 approaching oversold but a -1.40 bearish chart bias with a Bearish Marubozu — pure selling pressure, no support identified. DEXE has the best chart (+1.10 bias, Three White Soldiers) but RSI at 85 screams overbought after a 43% weekly run and the -13.5% daily drop suggests the reversal may already be underway with only 0.33x R/R. JTO is the most balanced candidate but the doji candle signals indecision and 1.05x R/R is below our 2x threshold. BTC dominance at 56.1% is actively suppressing alt performance. With Fear & Greed at 22 and trending down from 28 yesterday, the market hasn't found a floor yet. The Iran conflict and CPI report represent binary risk events that could push prices lower. Better to wait for capitulation washout or a confirmed reversal.
risk_factors: Iran conflict escalation could trigger a broader risk-off selloff across all crypto assets
CPI report could come in hot, pushing Fed rate hike expectations higher and crushing risk assets
U.S. government moving $288M in seized crypto to Coinbase Prime could signal upcoming selling pressure
watch_list: CPI inflation report release — hot print would be bearish, cool print could trigger relief rally
Fear & Greed index — watch for sub-15 capitulation level as potential contrarian buy signal
BTC price action at $60,000 psychological support — a bounce there could set up alt entries
market_context: Crypto markets are in Extreme Fear (22/100) with BTC at $62.5K facing dual headwinds from Iran geopolitical tensions and an imminent U.S. inflation report. BTC dominance at 56.1% signals capital is fleeing alts for relative safety in Bitcoin.
context_banner: Extreme Fear with multiple macro risk events ahead — capital preservation is the highest-conviction trade right now.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 22 and dropping from 28 yesterday indicates the market hasn't bottomed yet — historically reliable signal says wait for stabilization before buying.
U.S. government moving $288M in seized BTC/ETH to Coinbase Prime creates potential sell-side overhang and market anxiety.
Iran conflict reigniting and CPI report looming are creating binary risk events that make new entries reckless.
No candidate is deeply oversold enough to warrant a contrarian entry — BONK at 33 is closest but chart structure is actively bearish with no support.
CLARITY Act gaining law enforcement endorsement signals positive regulatory trajectory long-term but provides no near-term price catalyst.
